title: spergula sativa
intro: Spergula sativa, also known as the common spurrey or corn spurrey, is a plant species that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a biennial or annual plant that is native to Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Spergula sativa include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce fever. Spergula sativa is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
